entry.title: Re: Shared contract
entry.content: Eric... one more doubt... is there a way to work with shared allowance without linked contract?

entry.title: Re: Learning new UX - blank Catalog Search no longer avai...
entry.content: I have to disagree with the thought that this new "focused search" option is easier for the end user. There just needs to be a general way of getting to the overall catalog without relying on someone to recommend something to you or you entering keywords just to gain access.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
entry.content: Great, @Gerhard_Welker Thanks for all the work you put in this.

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud / Implementation of Express Delivery Service
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
we have the requirement to check if its possible to implement Express Delivery Service in a Public Cloud setting. Specifically our customer wants to send their products to their customers via UPS or DHL. They also want to be able to assign DHL or UPS to a sales order or shipment. 
I am new to this topic and i would appreciate any help on how or even if we can implement this in the Public cloud.
Thanks for all responses in advance.

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: Re: Empty sales area BP tcode
entry.content:
Hi GitteGreibe,

Thanks for your response.
I've done this. The sales area has already been selected as you suggested. If you check, the designation of the selected areas appears. There is no field with the ID.

entry.title: Re: CPI connectivity with PostgreSQL on-prem using Cloud...
entry.content:
Apparently "postgresql" is a reserved word, we had the same problem and it worked when we changed the virtual host from postgresql to posgressql2.

Hope it helps

entry.title: how to print handling units in custom physical inventory form in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

Greetings!

In our project (S/4HANA Embedded EWM) we are developing the custom form for Physical inventory in EWM. But, we couldn't find the logic to print Handling units in the form. Can any one suggest the logic or any Function Module to print the handling units.

Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Sachin.

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Company logo not visible on Enable Now Template Custo...

Hi Dirk, thanks! I actually replaced SAP original Photo which was visible from the first place by our logo in png format - as recommended.

Followed your advice, I activated the show-logo option and still nothing has changed! Could there be another solution to this? 

Thanks a lot in advance for your help!

entry.title: The SuccessFactors connector – more secure than ever
entry.content:
The SuccessFactors connector, used for communication between SAP Identity Management and SuccessFactors systems, is now enhanced to support mutual Transport Layer Security (mTLS) authentication. This change is determined by the announced retirement plan for HTTP Basic Authentication method for accessing APIs in SAP SuccessFactors. For more information, see Deprecation of HTTP Basic Authentication for APIs.

The mutual Transport Layer Security (mTLS) establishes an encrypted TLS connection, in which both parties use X.509 certificates to authenticate and verify each other. It provides an additional layer of security and that is the reason to be proved as the more secure authentication option.

To take advantage of the new authentication method, follow the procedure below.

First, you have to generate a certificate. You have two options to accomplish this step – via SAP for Me, which we recommend, or via SAP Cloud Identity Services- Identity Authentication service.

If you decide to use SAP for me, you should execute the steps described in SAP Note 3469904 . After you have successfully generated a certificate, extract the certificate trust chain from it.

In case you choose to generate your certificate via SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication service, you should execute Step 6 from the procedure described in Add System as Administrator.

We have reached the moment to upload your certificate to your SAP SuccessFactors system. Login to the system and navigate to Admin Center > Security Center > X.509 Public Certificate Mapping. Select the Add button, complete the required fields and choose Save.

 For more information, refer to Upgrade to X.509 Certificate-Based Authentication for Incoming Calls.

After this is fulfilled, you should adapt your existing SuccessFactors repository. To get all new constants, you should re-import the com.sap.idm.connector.sfsf connector package.

Until now, only basic authentication was possible, so after the package import, your repository probably looks like this:

Once you switch to authentication with mTLS, you would no longer need the constants SFSF_PASSWORD and SFSF_USER. Instead, you should put the path to the newly created certificate and its password. At the end, your repository constants should look like this:

Congratulations, you are officially using the newly introduced authentication method for provisioning between SAP Identity Management and your SuccessFactors system.

For more details around this change, you can check Setting Up a SuccessFactors System.

Any question or feedback will be appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

 I am interested to upskill my skills to develop my career. I am interested to join SAP Digital Skills Initiative to strengthen my skills which will be helpful in my career progress. I hope through this Digital Skills Initiative, it will be a boost to my career. I am excited to join Digital Skills Initiative to upskill my skills in SAP. Thank You

entry.title: Rearrange fields in customer form in sap customer checkout
entry.content:
Hi,

is there a way to rearrange field order or position in the customer form ?
I know that this form is now accessible for plugin, but i don't know where to find any documentation about.

thanks for your help,

entry.title: Re: Inquiry Regarding Note 2469139 Resolve findings of co...
entry.content:
Hello

SAP S/4HANA supports two or more storage locations belonging to the same MRP Area.

During the migration, however, you need to run the conversion report that will convert all storage locations planned separately or excluded from MRP into an MRP Area assignment. This conversion might fail if two of those storage locations are assigned to the same MRP Area, and that is why SAP recommends to avoid having two storage locations belonging to the same MRP Area during the migration. 

If you want to have two storage locations in the same MRP Area, you need to test the conversion report very carefully, to make sure you won't have problems during the migration. 

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: How to enable users to work with the message dashboard
entry.content:
Hi Roger,

have a look at SAP note 3042682. The note fixes the issue with the missing role based authorizations for the message monitoring.

entry.title: Re: Learning new UX - blank Catalog Search no longer avai...
entry.content:
Hello Amelie Fidric,

you assume that every customer has the recommendations in use, which is not the case with us.

Unfortunately, the search function you have targeted is not the solution for us, as our employees do not work in this way and need a view of the entire catalogue. In addition, the filters mentioned only come into play once a search has already been carried out, so unfortunately this experience offers no added value here.

As the universal changeover is coming soon, I would like to ask you to provide a brief description of what is described in the RoadMap under "Ability to browse the catalogue" in "Enhanced search functionality in the integrated learning experience".

Can you please also let me know whether activating the personalised recommendations requires additional licences.
Thank you für your effort.

entry.title: Exercise in Learning Journey Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP causes endless requests
entry.content:
In Learning Journey Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP -> Exercise Adding custom business logic there is some error. When testing the app everything seems fine but when I want to create a new Risk then the app freezes due to thousands of error messages. I checked out the solution branch to make sure I didn't cause the error myself. 

https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-side-by-side-extension-learning-journey/tree/solution/3_adding_custom_business_logic

Error Message: Failed to drill-down into criticality, invalid segment: criticality (see screenshot)

What is causing the problem?

entry.title: SAP Build Backend App - Entity - List Field Type
entry.content: I am just learning SAP Build and looking at the Backend App section. I have just created an Entity and added a field type of List with item type of text, but how/where do i add the list values?

entry.title: Re: Using Document Management as a Content Repository with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
So if I understand correctly the only issue by using the application option is that you can't set the parameters ExternalId and isContentBridgeEnabled? That would seem like a very easy fix. 

Anyways, after creating the repository using your guide, I was however able to add this repository in the application option too to view the documents.

Just make sure that in the destination created in BTP when onboarding the repository, make sure the URL is your 'uri' from the service key with /browser behind it. In some accounts it was a ' dummy url' and in other accounts it did not create it and had to do it manually.  

Then in the application option, add a new external repository, use the ID you used for the onboarding and the destination from BTP and voila: now you can easily view the content of your repository integration option using the application option.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge

import os

wouldn't execute. In fact, the word "import" wasn't coloured purple, as it was with the week 3 import workbook.

My workaround was to include it in the next cell.

Weird!

entry.title: Re: NEW receiving BIN vs the GR-area bin

Did you check the process type ?If not, you may need to check destination storage bin field in process type.

Hope this will help 🙂

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
It was a malicious typo from Vitaliy.
The cell type was defined as "Raw". You just had to switch to python instead.

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Outcome of the Q2/2024 Review
entry.content:
Dear Customers and Partners, 

We would like to provide an update on the progress made regarding your requests submitted through the Customer Influence portal for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Although there has been no major release in this review period, we have been diligently working to address your requests.
In our ongoing commitment to improving our product with and for you, we have prioritized qualified requests while still giving due consideration to requests that did not meet the voting threshold, depending on strategic fit and capacity.
 
Q2/2024 Review Outcomes

For spotlights on requests addressed throughout the last review cycle, kindly check out the summary blog post for each SAP Continuous Influence session related to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

SessionReview Summary
SAP Central Business Configuration for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionComing soon
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Asset ManagementQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, CDS View Extraction Enablement See request status list
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Data Management and Data MigrationQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Enterprise Portfolio and Project ManagementQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, FinanceQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Private Edition, Environment, Health and SafetyQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Private Edition,
Group Reporting		Q2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, LocalizationQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ManufacturingQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Master Data MaintenanceQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services AutomationQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SalesQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ServiceSee request status list
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and ProcurementQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Supply Chain LogisticsQ2/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User ExperienceQ2/2024

Thank you for your continuous feedback!

entry.title: Re: Single Sign On to SAP Cloud Integration (CPI runtime) from an external Identity Provider
entry.content:
Hi, thank you for your post, it´s very interessting.

But I have a question, it´s possible to have token exchange directly to SAP CI runtime configured to use SAP IAS as default IDP and the token was provided by another IDP?

We have SAP IAS for SAP Products, Azure for others and using KeyCloak  to some applications. My application uses KeyCloak to authenticate through Azure using MFA, and I want to use that token to authenticate to SAP CI Runtime to bring me the Principal Propagation end-to-end.

It looks insane? This scenario works fine with the frontend application of SAP CI, but not for the runtime.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: I liked it! I was struggeling to know what was failing and when I noticed that... evil typo!

entry.title: Applying Clean Code Principles in SAP UI5 Development - Reality check
entry.content:
Recently I came across the "Clean SAP UI5" book, which focuses on adopting clean code principles that focus on maintaining code that is easy to read, understand, and maintain. This is where the concept of "Clean Code" comes into play.

In this blog, I would like to highlight the principles of clean code in SAP UI5 along with illustrative examples, and at the end, I will share some thoughts based on practical experience in projects😉.

 

 

So what is Clean Code?

Clean Code is a set of practices that aim to produce code that is easy to read, simple to understand, and effortless to maintain. Robert C. Martin  introduced these principles in his book "Clean Code: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship." These principles are universal and can be applied to any programming language or framework, including SAP UI5.

Why Clean Code in UI5?

SAP UI5 is a robust framework for building responsive, enterprise-level applications. Given its complexity and the collaborative nature of enterprise projects, following clean code principles becomes even more critical. Clean code in UI5 development can lead to:

  • Better Collaboration: Easy-to-read code facilitates better collaboration among team members.
  • Faster Onboarding: New developers can quickly understand and contribute to the codebase.
  • Reduced Bugs: Clear and well-structured code reduces the likelihood of errors.
  • Enhanced Maintainability: Easier to refactor and extend applications as requirements evolve.

Key Principles of Clean Code in UI5

  1. Meaningful Names

    • Be Descriptive: Use meaningful and descriptive names for variables, functions, and classes. Avoid abbreviations that are not universally understood.
    • Consistent Naming Conventions: Follow consistent naming conventions throughout your codebase. For instance, use camelCase for variables and functions, and PascalCase for classes.

 

// Bad Example
var p; // what does p stand for?

// Good Example
var productPrice;

 

  • Single Responsibility Principle

    • Function Focus: Each function should have a single responsibility or perform a specific task. This makes the code easier to understand and maintain.

  • Avoid Magic Numbers and Strings

    • Use Constants: Replace magic numbers and strings with named constants to provide context and make the code self-explanatory.

 

// Bad Example
if (user.age > 18) { /* logic for adults */ }

// Good Example
const LEGAL_ADULT_AGE = 18;
if (user.age > LEGAL_ADULT_AGE) { /* logic for adults */ }
​

 

  • Comment Judiciously

    • Useful Comments: Write comments that explain why the code exists, not what it does. Well-written code should be self-explanatory, reducing the need for excessive comments.

 

// Bad Example
// This function adds two numbers
function add(a, b) {
    return a + b;
}

// Good Example
// Adjusts the user balance after a transaction
function adjustUserBalance(user, amount) {
    user.balance += amount;
}
​

 

  • DRY Principle (Don't Repeat Yourself)

    • Reusability: Avoid duplicating code by creating reusable functions and components. This not only reduces redundancy but also centralizes logic for easier maintenance.

 

// Bad Example
function getUserFirstName(user) { return user.firstName; }
function getUserLastName(user) { return user.lastName; }

// Good Example
function getUserProperty(user, property) { return user[property]; }
​

 

  • Proper Error Handling

    • Graceful Degradation: Ensure that your code handles errors gracefully. Use try-catch blocks and provide meaningful error messages to help with debugging.

 

// Bad Example
var data = JSON.parse(input);

// Good Example
try {
    var data = JSON.parse(input);
} catch (error) {
    console.error("Invalid JSON input: ", error.message);
}
​

 

Clean Code in Action: A UI5 Example

Let's look at a simple SAP UI5 example to illustrate some of these principles. Assume we are building a function to retrieve and display product details. 

 

// Bad Example
function getProductDetails(id) {
    var oModel = new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel();
    oModel.loadData("/api/products/" + id, {}, false);
    if (oModel.getData().price > 100) {
        alert("Price is over 100");
    }
    var oView = sap.ui.getCore().byId("myView");
    oView.setModel(oModel);
}

// Good Example
const API_ENDPOINT = "/api/products/";
const HIGH_PRICE_THRESHOLD = 100;

function getProductDetails(productId) {
    const oModel = new sap.ui.model.json.JSONModel();
    const productUrl = `${API_ENDPOINT}${productId}`;

    oModel.loadData(productUrl, {}, false);
    displayHighPriceAlert(oModel.getData().price);
    setViewModel("myView", oModel);
}

function displayHighPriceAlert(price) {
    if (price > HIGH_PRICE_THRESHOLD) {
        alert("Price is over " + HIGH_PRICE_THRESHOLD);
    }
}

function setViewModel(viewId, model) {
    const oView = sap.ui.getCore().byId(viewId);
    oView.setModel(model);
}

 

Reality Check Based on My Experience 🤔

While the principles of clean code are ideal for creating maintainable and robust code, the reality of software development can often make it challenging to adhere strictly to these guidelines. Here are some common obstacles developers might face:

  1. Time Pressure: Tight deadlines and the need for quick delivery can force developers to prioritize speed over code quality. In such cases, writing clean code might take a back seat to deliver functional code quickly.
  2. Changing Requirements: Frequent changes in project requirements can lead to rushed updates and patches, resulting in code that is less clean and harder to maintain. This can cause technical debt that accumulate over time.
  3. Lack of Experience: Junior developers or those new to a specific framework like SAP UI5 might struggle to apply best practices and clean code principles consistently. Limited knowledge and experience can make it difficult to write clean, maintainable code.

From my perspective, it's essential to find a balance between ideal practices and practical constraints. and do so Here are a few tips:

  • Incremental Improvement: Aim to improve the codebase incrementally. Start with the most critical areas and gradually apply clean code principles.
  • Code Reviews: Conduct regular code reviews to share knowledge and identify areas for improvement. This helps maintain a high standard of code quality and provides learning opportunities.
  • Peer Programming: Take the chance to learn from other perspectives by getting involved in peer programming with other developers 
  • Automated Tools: Use automated tools for code analysis and linting to enforce coding standards and catch common issues early some IDE's like VScode are offering this possibility by adding the relevant extensions.
  • Documentation: Maintain good documentation to support code understanding and maintenance, especially when clean code practices cannot be fully adhered to due to external pressures.

Final Word

Applying clean code principles to SAP UI5 development is essential for building maintainable, scalable, and robust applications. However, it's important to recognize that real world constraints can make it challenging to follow these principles perfectly. By understanding these constraints and striving for continuous improvement, developers can still achieve a high standard of code quality that benefits both the team and the end product.

Happy coding!  🤗

entry.title: CCLM - Decommission Cockpit
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have started collecting usage data using SCMON/UPL using CCLM.  Now we are in the process of analyzing decommission cockpit. lets say we started the jobs which collect data in Jan. We created a new analysis in decommission cockpit with option Analysis Period 13 months and wait time period 6 months.  This analysis shows some n number of objects to be decommissioned. My doubt is when should  we start looking at the object list? Will the object list get updated as I have provided  6 months waiting period to check if the object is used anytime in next 6 months?

 

Job Start Date : Jan

Analysis in Decommissioning cockpit - Jan 

When should we check object list in cockpit and start actually deleting the objects?

entry.content:
Thanks a lot @Subhasree  for organizing  such an educative event

Also special thanks to other volunteer too such as @meghdeep biswas

@sandeep @pradipta saha and others too

entry.title: [SAP MDK] Retrieving Mobile's Domain name of the host
entry.content:
Hello MDK Community,

I am currently developing an application using the SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) and I am facing a challenge in retrieving the host name (of mobile devices) during runtime.

The objective is to programmatically determine the host name of the device within my MDK application. This information is critical to implement custom logic for environment-specific routing.

Thus far, I have:

  • Reviewed MDK client API reference for any mention of network or host information.
  • Searched through various online examples and SAP community threads without much luck.

Additionally, I'm aware that for the WebApp, it's possible to retrieve the host by using the following JavaScript line:

const hostnameElements = window.location.hostname;
//output: ‘xxxxx-dev-mobile-appId-12345678.cfapps.euxx.hana.ondemand.com'

However, this method work only on WebApp and fail to function on mobile devices, as it's not compatible with NativeScript used by the MDK on mobile platforms.
What I need is an alternative method that works on MDK clients within a mobile environment.

To provide some background on the issue: The purpose of retrieving the hostname is to determine the environment in which the app is running. For instance, if the hostname contains the term "dev," then the app should open a specific URL associated with the Development environment. Similarly, if the hostname contains "qas" or "prod," it should open a link tailored for the QAS or Prod environments respectively.

If anyone has insights on accessing the mobile host information in an MDK environment or knows of any workaround to achieve a similar result as window.location.hostname on the web, your guidance would be greatly valued.

For reference, I'm using SAP Mobile Services for the backend orchestration and the latest MDK client version.
Thank you in advance for your assistance!

Best regards, Khaled

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Planning Area extension for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Thank you for this blog, very informative! While the dynamic extension on stories is a great addition, unfortunately unless it also dynamically expands when you run data actions the use case is not really possible with our customers in my experience. For example, the user will want to make an entry in a table, and then run a data action before publishing (where the data action will often have steps outside of the planning range naturally).

Is it on the roadmap for the DAs to also dynamically extend?

Thanks!

David

entry.title: LSMW recording using TCODE MMCC is not capturing MATNR field
entry.content: We are in the process of replicating around 10000 materials which exist in multiple plants. If the number range is internal, I'm having no issues. But for materials with external number range, I'm not able to capture the MATNR field in MMCC transaction even after entering a value in the field during the recording. Any idea how to get this done. A quick reply would be much appreciated.

Maria

entry.title: Renumber G/L Accounts App - use the development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL).
entry.content:
When we try to Renumber G/L Accounts App  in out Partner Test and development system customising tenant, we get error:

To create a mapping, use the development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL).

Could you please help us to find a solution how to renumber G/L accounts?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Renumber G/L Accounts App - use the development syste...
entry.content:
Refer the below KBA:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003289450

It is supported only in Development-Customizing tenant for 3SL. Your system is partner Demo customizing.

Starter or Partner Demo Customizing/Developments Tenants are not supported for G/L account numbering.

entry.title: Configuration for additional information in Manage workforce In S/4 Hana public colud

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for additional information in Manage wo...
entry.content: Additional Academic details can be configured the through the SSCUI -'Maintain Academic Titles' (SSCUI ID 103437 )

entry.title: Re: Partner Speed Dating Session
entry.content: However, for those seeking alternatives that provide a more structured and user-friendly experience, DoULike is a noteworthy option. DoULike caters to a wide range of dating preferences, including serious relationships and casual dating that way, with a focus on compatibility and ease of use. Unlike Doublelist, which primarily focuses on personal ads, DoULike employs advanced matching algorithms to help users find partners who truly match their interests and goals. Whether you're looking for casual connections or something more meaningful, DoULike offers a reliable and effective alternative to Doublelist, ensuring a safer and more personalized dating experience.

entry.title: Demo Data in S4Hana
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

We just activated a new instance of S4HANA cloud, and my team is looking to populate it with demo data similar to what is available in the trial, as we are a new shop without a lot of historical data.

Does anyone know where I could find demo data? I found a few configuration templates, but I'm looking for actual records in order to figure out the integrations with some of our home grown systems. 

Thanks!

entry.title: Different Optimizer in Deep learning and when it need to be used?
entry.content:
Today's topic is to different Optimizer function in Deep learning and when it need to be used. I will discuss about the most used optimizer with real scenario where it can be used.

1. ADAMS : Adaptive Moment Estimation algorithm  is build with  combination of the gradient descent with momentum algorithm and the RMSP algorithm. When the data volume is large  with a no of parameters ,the if we want efficient computation with fast speed then we will use ADAMS. It also rectifies vanishing learning rate, high variance. Only problem is it is costly.

2. RMS Prop (Root Mean Square) :  This method used an Exponential Weighted Average to determine the learning rate at time t for each iteration. Suggested to set gamma at 0.95 in formula, as it has been showing good results for most of the cases. We can use it when the data set contains non-stationary objectives or when training recurrent neural networks(RNNs). It has been shown to perform well on tasks where the Adagrad method's performance is compromised due to its continually decreasing learning rates.

3. Gradient Decent : This algorithm calculates based on the first order derivative of a loss function and  calculates that which way the weights should be change to reach a minimum. Through backpropagation, the loss is transferred from one layer to another and the model’s parameters also known as weights are modified depending on the losses so that the loss can be minimized. 

It is the most used optimization algorithm used in linear regression and classification algorithms using back propagation.

 

4. Stochastic Gradient Descent : In this optimization instead of using the entire dataset for each iteration in back propagation process , only a single random training dataset  is selected to calculate the gradient (loss) and update the model parameters ( Weight and Bias). This random selection of dataset  introduces randomness.

When the dataset is large and need to adjust weight and bias using backpropagation method we can use Stochastic Gradient Descent and it will useful . The equation is -

5 Adagrad : In this algorithm we adapts the learning rates of individual parameters by scaling them based on the historical squared gradients of those parameters. These algorithm se different learning rates for each parameter base on iteration. The reason behind the need for different learning rates is that the learning rate for sparse features parameters needs to be higher compare to the dense features parameter because the frequency of occurrence of sparse features is lower.

It is useful for sparse data as it ensures that infrequent features, which are common in text data, receive larger updates, improving model performance.

All the above are mostly used optimizer used in deep learning. I provided only the summarized format which help to understand only.  You can get the details in different sites.

Hope this will help.

entry.title: Removed lock object from Transport request by mistake
entry.content:
Hello all,

I had created a table lock object(from SE11) which had moved to the Test environment. Due to a change the lock object was not needed anymore and hence I deleted it in the development environment and the deletion reference of the object got created in the new transport request in the Development environment. So ideally when the new transport request moves the lock object should get deleted from the Test environment.

While checking the transport request, by mistake I deleted the deletion reference of the lock object from the new transport request. Upon realising my mistake, I immediately recreated it with the same name and deleted it and saved it in the transport request again , so that again the reference is back in the transport request. 

My question is, once the transport request moves to the Test environment will the lock object get deleted from Test environment?

I am worried because the creation instances are different now though name is same.

Please advise. Many thanks

entry.content:
Hi Experts,

have to ask again because may post from last year had not been answered.
We have a curriculum with requirements. When the requirements are fullfilled they are shown an completed, but the curriculum is still uncompleted.

What can we make wrong?

 

 

 

 

 

 

Regards 
Guido

entry.title: How to read AIF logs from ABAP code(RAP) in S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to fetch the log messages from AIF Message dashboard app on S4 HANA public cloud.

We are developing a custom report using RestFul ABAP (RAP ) and we are stuck at a place how to read the error log messages for an asynchronous API to know the status of Asset created in the system.

( API- https://api.sap.com/api/FIXEDASSETCREATEMAIN/overview )

We are able to see the AIF logs in the message monitoring( Message dashoard ) fiori apps, but the same AIF message we would want to fetch into custom ABAP RAP code.

Could not find relevant information on the community , hence posting this question.

Appreciate if some one  provide a solution how to achieve this.

regards,

Kumar

entry.title: About the SAP Companion Help icon (Question Mark) on Target Application
entry.content:

However, recently we witnessed a weird issue that the Help icon sometimes disappears, and does not load. Later, suddenly, we see that Help icon. 

I checked the Workarea settings; everything seems to be fine. Any reason why this happens? Any known solution for this?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Fundamentals of Security in BTP: Implement Authentication and Authorization in a Node.js App
entry.content:
Hi @Raja_Gupta ,

I just finished reading your blog on security aspects in BTP, OAuth, and the use of approuter for authentication and authorization in Node.js using XSUAA. It was incredibly insightful and well-explained—thank you for sharing your expertise!

I have a question about scope checking in your Node.js example. You used 'checkScope' for this purpose, but I want to know if there's a way to directly access the scope information from the 'req.authInfo' (For example : To determine what scopes the user has). Could you share how this might be done, if possible?

Thanks again for such an informative and helpful post!

Regards,
Poomathi K

entry.title: Is it possible to adjust dimensions of shipping unit/handlng unit in WM outbound delivery order?
entry.content:
How to change the dimensions of shipping unit/handling unit in an outbound delivery order (WH managed Storage Location)?

We are using Pack Handling Units – App
This App displays the details of the HU, but there is no possibility of changing them. 
Where would SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition offer a possibility to alter the dimensions, when using the 'Pack Outbound Deliveries' or 'Pack Handling Units' App?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to adjust dimensions of shipping unit/...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

You cannot change the dimensions of a shipping unit or handling unit directly in the ‘Pack Outbound Deliveries’ or ‘Pack Handling Units’ apps. These apps are used to pack products into handling units based on the packing instructions. The dimensions of the handling units are determined by the packaging material specified in the packing instructions. 

 If you want to change the dimensions of a handling unit, you need to adjust the dimensions of the packaging material in the packing instructions by the ‘Manage Packing Instructions’ app.

Please note that any changes to the packing instructions will affect all future handling units that use this packing instruction. If you only want to change the dimensions for a specific outbound delivery order, you might need to create a new packing instruction with the desired dimensions and assign it to the product for this specific order.

Please check further information about this APP in the links below: 

Manage Packing Instructions APP

Manage Packing Instructions - Master Data

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Removed lock object from Transport request by mistake
entry.content:
Yes, it should take care of removing the lock object when the transport is imported.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud / Implementation of Express...
entry.content:
Hello LukasKuhn . SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) extensions offers functionality to build extension applications where you can integrate with external applications such as Express Delivery Service DHL etc. Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP  

Extensibility and Integration are two core functions that help you digitize your business and adapt your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution. Integration can be enabled through the SAP  Cloud Platform Integration service or other third-party applications.  You can learn more with Documentation

In this scenario you can integrate these external delivery systems like DHL via API calls or services and assign them to sales orders or shipments.

You can set up  integration that can allow pull and push functionality with real-time order tracking, providing rates, printing shipping labels, etc .  As example you can pull information from DHL Express - MyDHL API

You can then update information in S/4HANA Cloud. You can refer to Sales API Documentation

Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) API to update sales orders from external systems for information.

Configuration will be  needed to set this express delivery service  update which would typically depend upon the business requirement. This process would likely involve a  customization as per the business needs and the functionality required.

You can also check the other API like Manage Outbound Deliveries

SAP Integration Suite  is platform-as-a-service that helps businesses seamlessly connect and integrate their applications, data, and processes within their organization and beyond. You can review the available integration packs and customize it to accelerate integration development with out-of-the-box integration packs for end-to-end scenarios.

SAP Integration Suite is available in your trial account, SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Demo Data in S4Hana
entry.content:
Hello Petez. SAP offers you pre-configured solutions with SAP Best Practices, (Documentation) which you can explore based on demo data before adapting and launching them. Specific information on the implementation process and the phase-specific configuration activities with a list of accelerators about configuration with SSCUIs are available in the SAP Activate Roadmap viewer Information published on SAP site.

Configuration Experts can demonstrate the solution processes using the Starter system Customizing Tenant. Learn more with documentation on SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer. Pre-packaged data exist in starter system that  helps new users to explore different system scenarios and capabilities. However, the specifics of accessing this demo data depend on your subscription type and individual SAP S/4HANA Cloud configuration.

You can also learn about the ways on how you can bring your legacy data migration in development tenant with Documentation. purpose of this deliverable is to develop, test, and execute the data migration programs and processes defined in the Explore phase

 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Is Amazon RDS database supported SAP Data Services 4.3 SP2 as repository or source/target DB?
entry.content:
Hi,
We are migrating to AWS Cloud and would like to use Amazon RDS for Data Stores and SAP Data Services Repository, CMS Repository and Audit Repository. Is there option for support some database from RDS portfolio or is there plan for supporting of any Database from portfolio


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is Amazon RDS database supported SAP Data Services 4....
entry.content:
You can refer the SAP Data Services 4.3 PAM  to see all the available repositories and datastore connections supported with SAP Data Services 4.3.

You can refer our https://roadmaps.sap.com/ as it has all the next features in pipeline that are planned to release. If the Amazon RDS support is planned in future you can see the details in the data services roadmap.

If you wish to have this additional feature of having RDS database support with sap data services, you can also create an enhancement request with our enhancement team to have this feature- ##2737627 - How To: Enhancement Request Process for SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward

entry.title: SAP AI Assistant Tool for On Premise Insurance Claims Management Solution
entry.content:
Hello,

We are in the initial phases of discussing the development of a new Assistant tool for SAP Insurance Claims Management business users. Our goal is to enhance productivity and efficiency in handling claims within the insurance sector. We aim to build an on-premise UI tool that can interact with users and provide useful information, including text, audio, and video content about the product, and trigger business processes with the click of a button. For example, user types a text and on the action 'Send' we trigger a business event in the background. Additionally, the tool should help automate repetitive tasks and assist users in making informed business decisions by offering quick, automated support when needed.

Given our objectives, we are exploring whether SAP already has tools that we could leverage for building a framework that allows AI automated rules and business content that can be integrated with on-premise SAP solutions.

After some research, I found an article mentioning that SAP is planning to release the SAP Joule Assistant across all applications. Will SAP Joule be available for SAP for Insurance solutions as well? SAP Prepares to Add Joule Generative AI Copilot Across Its Apps

As I am new to this topic and at the beginning of my SAP AI journey, any suggestions or guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thanks and regards,

Arun

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: How to capture Manager History in Performance Forms w...
entry.content:
I've opened a support ticket and had a discussion with one of the SF Performance & Goals experts, unfortunately it is not a defect but just the way the system works. The following is a summary in the ticket of the issue and explanation, in case anyone else has the same question:

The scenario at issue:
A performance form has a goal section configured to Include the ability to rate - Everyone shares one rating box for each item and Except the employee who has a self-assessment rating box.
The manager has write permission for the official rating (Item Rating)
The employee has write permission for the unofficial rating (Unofficial User Rating)
Manager A enters ratings for the goals during the evaluation step
There is a manager change late in the review cycle and the form is routed to the new manager, Manager B
When Manager B opens the form, the ratings entered by Manager A are shown, there is no indication that the ratings were not entered by Manager B

  • For the purpose of this discussion, item ratings are ratings for individual items in a ratable section. Items are goals in a Goal section or competencies in a Competency section.
  • Ratable sections have official ratings (Item Rating) and unofficial ratings (Unofficial User Rating or Subject Rating). 
  • Each item has 1 item rating, it is linked to the item not the user who entered the rating. The item rating is not owned by a user or role, it it the rating of record in the system. 
  • Each item can have multiple unofficial ratings, unofficial ratings are linked to a specific user. Unofficial ratings are owned by the user who enters the rating, a user cannot change the unofficial rating of another user. 
  • Which ratings are available will depend on the rating option and form permissions. 
    • Rating Option 0: Everyone has access to the official rating, it is the only rating available. 
    • Rating Option 1: Everyone has access to the official rating. Additionally, the employee can enter an unofficial rating
    • Rating Option 2: Everyone has access to the official rating. Everyone can also enter their individual unofficial ratings
    • Rating Option 3: Only the direct manager (EM) can access the official rating. Only the employee can access the unofficial rating. No other users/roles have access to a rating field but can see the employee and manager's ratings in the Ratings from Other's tab. 
      • See Implementing and Managing Performance Management - Rating Options for details about rating options.

So in this scenario, it isn't that the ratings now belong to Manager B, it is that the form is showing the official rating of record for those goals. Manager B just sees them because they are now the manager. The "Rating by Meghan..." label is showing because the section is configured to use the EZ-Rater UI.

  • Implementing and Managing Performance Management>Enabling Form Features>EZ Rater
    • When EZ Rater is enabled, the rating labels of official rating (item-rating) are defined differently for the form and the PDF and print version.
      • For the form, the label is defined by FB_UI_SPEED_RATER_OFFICIAL_RATING (for the employee view), split_rating_title_format_no_colon (for the manager view), and TEXT_BY_USER in the language package.
        • It's shown as Rating by {manager name} for managers and Official Rating for employees and other users.

 

I've submitted an enhancement ticket to see if we can at least report on "Last modified by" for the Official Rating and would appreciate any votes if you think this would be helpful to you: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/325427 

Thank you to everyone!
Geena

entry.title: SAP Hana Running Hyper-v Windows Server 2019 or 2022
entry.content:

"https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/1409608"

Can anyone provide a KB or PDF for reading?

Thanks

entry.title: About using QR Code for Published Content in SAP Enable Now, and Published Content Responsiveness
entry.content:
When we publish any content object (Simulation, Book, SAP Companion Tour, etc.) in SAP Enable Now Manager, we see the QR Code option displayed for the published content. Wanted to check that when a user accesses this QR code from any device, will the content play on all types of devices (Desktops, Laptops, Tablets, etc.)? We have not tested this, but may get a requirement for the same. 

  • Simulations and Books open in the content player of SAP Enable Now. I think the player name is "Trainer". Here, will these simulations play in all types of devices (Desktops, Laptops, Tablets, etc.) as responsive content?
  • SAP Companion content (e.g., Guided Tours) play directly on the live target system (e.g., SAP S4). In this case, when the user accesses the QR code for this SAP Companion content, will the content play in all types of devices (Desktops, Laptops, Tablets, etc.) as responsive content? Here in this case, I assume that the target live system (e.g., SAP S4) is responsive as well.

Are there guidelines about using QR for published content, plus the responsiveness of published SEN and SAP Companion content?

entry.title: Re: How to generate purchase order output on change of a purchase order?
entry.content:
Many thanks for the information!

If the processing of PURCHASE_ORDER is set to "Immediately", the output with the change is also sent immediately. 

Is there a possibility to adjust the time of the change output? If change output, then to "Scheduled" so that you can view the output beforehand and discard it if necessary?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Company logo not visible on Enable Now Template Custo...
entry.content: Check that the object for your company logo has been published in your Workarea.

entry.title: Re: About the SAP Companion Help icon (Question Mark) on...
entry.content: My guess is either there is no help for the app and noHelpMode=hideButton, or you're using serviceLayerVersion=SEN (or WPB) and its an authentication issue.

entry.title: Re: About using QR Code for Published Content in SAP Enab...
entry.content:
The QR Code is just capturing the URL. That URL will open the Trainer in the browser on whatever device you are on. There is currently NO special formatting for the type of device. The best you have is the Zoom for Mobile Playback setting in the Playback Settings, which you'll probably want to set to Zoom to Fit.

SAP Companion embedded help is slightly different and is now supported on mobile devices (it will reformat for the smaller form size) but simulations / Book Pages accessed via the Learning page will not.

entry.title: Financial Accounting Certification Options - Please compare C_TS4FI & C_S4CFI
entry.content:
Appreciate guidance for certification at the intermediate level for Financial Accounting. 

Kindly descibe the difference between C_TS4FI & C_S4CFI.  Is the differentiating factor one is Cloud Private Edition and one is Cloud Public Edition, and if true, which is recommended for an SAP Consultant role. Also, are these considered beginner level certifications, and are there any intermediate certifications or is the next level only Professional?  

Are there any other FICO certifications available I have missed?

Thank you!! Greatly appreciate the insight!

entry.title: Re: Know more about SAP IAS SCIM APIs - Latest
entry.content:
@timsen ,

Yes, I had a difficult time changing the password status value, also.  It had a hard time navigating to the "status" attribute of the [passwordDetails] object. I needed the explicit full path.

Here is a PATCH body that worked for me, finally:

{
   "schemas": [
      "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp",
      "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"
      ],
   "Operations": [
      {
         "op": "replace",
         "path": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User:passwordDetails.status",
         "value": "enabled"
      }
   ]
}

entry.title: Re: SAP PM - PP Integration Planned Maintenance & Capacity Planning

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Know more about SAP IAS SCIM APIs - Latest
entry.content:
Sorry, @timsen .

The difference between these was pretty minor!!

urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User.passwordDetails.status

urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User:passwordDetails.status
